title: Lemon Pepper Chicken
author: Family
description: Juicy, golden lemon pepper chicken with a bright crust — built for the flat-top.
ingredients: 2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs (or breasts)
1 tbsp avocado or canola oil (for cooking)
steps: Whisk together 2 lemons, juiced, 1 tbsp lemon zest, 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tsp coarsely ground black pepper, 4 garlic cloves, minced, 1 tsp kosher salt, 1 tsp Dijon mustard, 1 tsp honey, and 1 tsp fresh thyme leaves (optional) in a bowl until combined.

Add 2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs (or breasts) to a zip-lock bag or shallow dish. Pour marinade over the chicken and coat well. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes — 2 to 4 hours is ideal. Don't exceed 8 hours or the acid will start breaking down the texture.

Remove chicken from the marinade and pat lightly dry with paper towels — this is key for getting a good sear. Discard the used marinade. Let the chicken rest at room temp for about 10 minutes while you heat the griddle.
Preheat your flat-top griddle to medium-high (around 400–425°F). Add 1 tbsp avocado or canola oil (for cooking) and spread it across the cooking surface. You want it shimmering but not smoking.

Lay chicken flat on the griddle — don't crowd it. For thighs: cook 5–6 minutes per side undisturbed until a deep golden crust forms. For breasts: cook 4–5 minutes per side, then press gently with a spatula to ensure even contact. Avoid moving the chicken around — let the crust develop.
Pull the chicken when it hits 160°F internal — it will carry over to 165°F while resting. Rest for 5 minutes before slicing. This keeps the juices locked in.
notes: Thighs vs breasts: Thighs are more forgiving on a flat-top — more fat means more moisture and better crust. Breasts work great too but watch your temp closely. Don't skip the pat dry: moisture on the surface = steam, not sear. Paper towels make the difference between golden and grey. Honey watch: the honey in the marinade caramelizes fast — if you see it darkening too quickly, drop to medium heat. Batch cooking: this marinade scales easily, great for meal prepping. Slice leftovers for salads, wraps, or rice bowls.

title: Chicken Tortilla Soup
author: Family
description: A hearty, brothy chicken tortilla soup with black beans, corn, and lime, topped with crispy homemade tortilla strips and avocado.
ingredients: 14 oz can black beans, drained and rinsed
14 oz can corn, drained and rinsed
1/2 cup cilantro, chopped, divided (reserve 1/4 for garnish)
1 lime, cut into wedges, to serve
steps: Tortilla strips: Preheat a pan with 1/4 cup oil over medium-high heat. Cut tortillas into thin strips and fry them in batches in the hot oil until crisp. Remove from the pan and let them drain on a paper towel. Repeat with the remaining strips, adding more oil as needed, then set aside.
Soup: Preheat a pot with 1 Tbsp oil over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion, garlic, and jalapeño and sauté until the veggies soften.
Add the whole chicken breasts, corn, beans, chili powder, cumin, crushed tomatoes, salt, 1/4 cup of the cilantro, and chicken broth. Bring to a boil and let simmer for at least 25 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the pot and shred it using 2 forks. Add the shredded chicken back to the soup and simmer another 5 minutes, then stir in the lime juice.
Serve the soup with tortilla strips, pieces of avocado, the reserved fresh cilantro, and lime wedges.
notes: Reserve 1/4 of the chopped cilantro for garnish. Add the lime juice at the end so it stays bright.
